title: Broad Closeout Validation Intent Separation
goal: Resolve #406 by making broad-work closeout separate validation proof, slice landing, lane satisfaction, and larger-intent closure in compact review output.
non_goals: Do not create a scoring system for intent satisfaction.
Do not make validation less strict.

intent.outcome: Broad work closeout exposes validation proof and intent satisfaction as separate answers, and validation success alone is not sufficient closure evidence.
intent.constraints: Reuse planning/report records where possible; avoid a new durable surface unless the compact report cannot carry the signal.
intent.latitude: Tighten compact closeout_trust fields, summary projections, or focused tests as needed.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the fix needs a new satisfaction scoring system or broad planning archive rewrite.

execution.next_step: Inspect current closeout_trust and intent satisfaction output, then tighten compact fields/tests.
execution.proof: Run focused closeout/report tests, package proof selector, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, and reconcile.

invariants: Validation commands remain strict proof, not intent satisfaction.
Slice, lane, and larger-intent closure remain separately visible.
No new scoring system or replacement for human review is introduced.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Improve weak-agent recovery and strong-agent leverage for package lifecycle and closeout workflows.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Broad closeout/report output now separates validation proof, requested slice, lane/system intent, and larger-intent closure in closeout_trust.intent_satisfaction_check.closure_scope.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Parent issue review may remain if live GitHub parent has additional unsliced expectations.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The package proof selector correctly flagged direct src/agentic_workspace/cli.py edits for review; this edit is runtime report payload work rather than command-definition semantics.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for #406; inspect parent #403 before claiming the broader weak-agent lane closed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Re-evaluate the parent weak-agent lane and close or replan it based on live issue state.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest and implement all issues lane by lane. Commit and push after each milestone. Collect dogfooding evidence.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Complete the remaining weak-agent recovery child slice with checked-in planning, proof, and honest parent-lane continuation.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Implement #406 as a compact closeout/review signal hardening slice.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm validation proof, slice landed, lane/system intent, and larger-intent closure are distinct in compact output.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: workspace CLI closeout/report output/tests and planning evidence surfaces for #406
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused closeout/report tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, reconcile
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any new satisfaction scoring system, new durable closeout surface, or broad archive migration.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Planning package archive algorithms unless the compact workspace report cannot expose the needed distinction.

context_budget.live working set: issue #406, closeout_trust output, summary projections, tests/test_workspace_cli.py, src/agentic_workspace/cli.py
context_budget.recoverable later: GitHub issue body and archived plan closeout
context_budget.externalize before shift: Keep this plan updated with proof and closeout state.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Active slice #406: separate validation proof from intent satisfaction in broad closeout.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Need for scoring, new surface, or broad planning archive rewrite.

references.label: Make validation-vs-intent satisfaction explicit in broad work closeout

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make broad-work closeout distinguish proof success from intent satisfaction.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No scoring system, no human-review replacement, and no weakening of validation proof.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Which compact report fields/tests to tighten, as long as validation and closure semantics are clearly separated.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The implementation needs a new durable closeout surface, scoring system, or broad archive migration.

active_milestone.scope: Compact closeout/review signal hardening and focused tests.

immediate_next_action: Inspect closeout_trust fields and add focused tests/fields that separate proof, slice, lane, and larger intent.
invariants: Validation commands remain strict proof, not intent satisfaction.
Slice, lane, and larger-intent closure remain separately visible.
No new scoring system or replacement for human review is introduced.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "closeout_trust or intent_satisfaction or report_surfaces_package_workflow_evidence"
uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed tests/test_workspace_cli.py src/agentic_workspace/cli.py --format json
uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Broad work closeout exposes validation proof and intent satisfaction as separate answers.
Reviewers can see whether closure is for the slice, the lane, or the larger intent.
Validation success alone is not presented as sufficient closure evidence.
The compact signal is covered by tests.

execution_run.what happened: Added closeout_trust.intent_satisfaction_check.closure_scope with separate answers for validation proof, requested slice, lane/system intent, and larger-intent closure. Added a report fixture where proof passes while follow-on remains required.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI report payload, workspace CLI tests, planning state, and active execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "closeout_trust or intent_satisfaction or report_surfaces_package_workflow_evidence"; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed tests/test_workspace_cli.py src/agentic_workspace/cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/broad-closeout-validation-intent-separation.plan.json --format json; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: #406 completed; parent #403 remains the next weak-agent lane review surface.
execution_run.next step: Archive #406, commit and push, close #406, then inspect parent #403.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Ingest and implement all issues lane by lane. Commit and push after each milestone. Collect dogfooding evidence.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #406 implementation landed and tests prove closeout_trust separates validation proof from intent closure; parent #403 was later closed after #404-#408 child evidence was reviewed.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Validation should prove the slice, not silently replace larger intent satisfaction.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer compact review fields over new surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can a reviewer see what passed, what landed, and whether the larger intent is actually closed?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #406 is complete, and parent #403 was closed after all named child issues landed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A broad closeout can still claim validation proof as sufficient intent closure or parent #403 contains additional unmet expectations.
